UiKitComponentSvelte.keywords: uikit, svelte-component, data-attributes, css-tokens, file-structure, a11y, token-overrides

BeliefState.Axiom:
CSS custom properties (design tokens) are inherited through `:root` in `.tokens.css`. Shadow DOM is NOT used for token inheritance â€” the component relies on the global CSS cascade.
The tokens file has two mandatory blocks: 1. `:root { ... }` â€” the token declarations block. 2. Override block â€” tokens where the SVG value differs from the VKUI default, each with a `/* override: was Xpx */` comment.
The CSS module (`.module.css`) consumes these tokens via `var(--vkui--...)` functions. No hardcoded pixel values in `.module.css` â€” every dimension, color, and spacing comes from a token.
Counter element: `box-sizing: border-box` is mandatory (prevents overflow when the counter number grows).

Every uikit component consists of EXACTLY four files, all in kebab-case, all in a single directory `src/ui//`:
| File | Purpose | |--------------------------|------------------------------------------------| | `.svelte` | Svelte component (data-attributes, a11y) | | `.module.css` | CSS module (data-attribute selectors) | | `.tokens.css` | `:root` + override tokens | | `.stories.ts` | Storybook stories (mirror of spec) |
No PascalCase (`Button.svelte`), no camelCase (`buttonModule.css`), no additional files (`button.utils.ts` belongs elsewhere or doesn't exist). The four-file set is the closed-world contract per `ComponentArtefact`.

Every interactive state that is normally expressed through a CSS pseudo-class (`:hover`, `:active`, `:disabled`) MUST also be expressed through a `data-state` attribute on the inner wrapper element. CSS pseudo-classes cannot be triggered programmatically in headless test environments â€” `data-state` provides the testable channel.
States: `normal`, `hover`, `active`, `disabled`. The `data-state` attribute is always present, never conditionally omitted. The component derives `data-state` from the `state` prop, not from browser events.
For test assertions: `expect(element).toHaveAttribute('data-state', 'hover')` â€” NOT `expect(element).toHaveStyle(...)` for pseudo-class-triggered styles (that won't work in headless).

Every interactive uikit component MUST declare: - `role` â€” WAI-ARIA role (button, checkbox, etc.). - `aria-label` â€” computed from label + counter (when present). - `aria-disabled` â€” bound to the disabled state. - `tabindex` â€” `0` when interactive, `-1` when disabled. - Keyboard support â€” Enter and Space for activation.
For Button specifically: - `role="button"` - `aria-label={label}` (plus counter if present: `` `${label} ${counter}` ``) - `aria-disabled={disabled}` - `aria-pressed={state === 'active'}` (optional, for toggle mode) - `tabindex={disabled ? -1 : 0}`
Focus styles use `:focus-visible` with appearance-dependent styling. No custom focus ring that overrides the browser default without providing equivalent visual feedback.

SVG icons have their viewBox cropped to the tight bounding box of the path â€” NOT the 20Ă—20 Figma container. This prevents unwanted whitespace around icons and ensures consistent spacing within the button.
The icon SVG is inlined (not an `` tag) so its fill color can be controlled via CSS. Icon fill inherits from the button's current appearance/mode color.

A uikit component receives its variant configuration through Svelte 5 `$props()` destructuring. The prop surface matches the spec's Variants table columns:
```typescript let { appearance = 'accent', mode = 'primary', size = 's', state = 'normal', icon = false, label = '', counter = undefined, indicator = false, disabled = false, }: Props = $props(); ```
The `disabled` prop takes precedence over `state` â€” when `disabled={true}`, `data-state` is `disabled` regardless of the `state` prop value. The component derives `aria-disabled` and `tabindex` from `disabled`.

CSS module selectors follow the data-attribute pattern consistently: - `.btn[data-size="s"]` for size-dependent styles. - `.btn[data-mode="primary"][data-appearance="accent"]` for mode Ă— appearance combinations. - `.btn[data-state="hover"]` for state-dependent styles. - Child elements use class selectors: `.icon`, `.label`, `.counter`, `.indicator`.
No ID selectors, no tag selectors (except `:root` in tokens), no descendant selectors deeper than 2 levels. Every selector is scoped to the component's root class (`.btn`).

Definitions.Definition: The closed-world set of four files that constitute one uikit component: `.svelte`, `.module.css`, `.tokens.css`, `.stories.ts`. All in `src/ui//`, all in kebab-case.

A CSS selector that targets a `data-*` HTML attribute on the component's inner wrapper: `.btn[data-mode="primary"]`. Preferred over class-based selectors because `data-*` attributes are machine-verifiable in tests via `toHaveAttribute`.

A CSS custom property declaration in `.tokens.css` whose value differs from the VKUI default. Must carry a `/* override: was ... */` comment. Originates from the Token Mapping table in the component's `.ui.spec.md`.

Pattern.Intent: Canonical structure of a uikit Svelte component file.
Pattern.Why: Single `$props()` destructure with all variant props; `$derived` resolves state vs disabled; inner wrapper carries all data-attributes and ARIA; child elements conditionally rendered; no `:host()`, no ``, no legacy patterns.

Pattern.Intent: Canonical structure of a uikit `.tokens.css` file.
Pattern.Why: Single `:root` block with all token declarations; override tokens carry `/* override: was ... */` comments; token names match the VKUI naming convention; no shadow DOM, no `@import`, no extra blocks.

Pattern.Intent: Canonical structure of a uikit `.module.css` file.
Pattern.Why: Data-attribute selectors for variant-dependent styles; CSS custom properties for all values; `box-sizing: border-box` on counter; no `:host()`, no hardcoded values, no deep nesting.

AntiPattern.WhyBad: `:host()` compound selectors fail in headless Chromium (`AX_DATA_ATTRS_NOT_HOST`). Padding returns `0px` from `getComputedStyle`, breaking visual tests. The inner-wrapper approach is the proven alternative.
AntiPattern.Good: Inner wrapper with data-attribute: `.btn[data-mode="primary"] { background: var(--vkui--color_background_accent); }`.

AntiPattern.Bad: Component relies on CSS `:hover` pseudo-class for hover styling, with no `data-state` attribute.
AntiPattern.WhyBad: Headless test environments cannot trigger `:hover` programmatically (`AX_DATA_STATE_FOR_TESTABILITY`). The hover variant is untestable â€” visual regression tests pass trivially without actually verifying hover appearance.
AntiPattern.Good: Add `data-state={resolvedState}` attribute and use `.btn[data-state="hover"]` selectors. Tests assert `toHaveAttribute('data-state', 'hover')`.

AntiPattern.WhyBad: Token not consumed via custom property (`AX_CSS_TOKENS_VIA_ROOT`). The value cannot be overridden, themed, or traced back to the VKUI token system. When the design system updates the token, this component silently stays at the old value.
AntiPattern.Good: `height: var(--vkui--size_button_small_height--compact);` â€” the token from `.tokens.css` flows through.

AntiPattern.WhyBad: Violates kebab-case convention (`AX_FILE_STRUCTURE_KEBAB_CASE`, D-UC03). Inconsistent with existing project files (`app.svelte`, `app.stories.ts`). Breaks the closed-world `ComponentArtefact` contract.

AntiPattern.Bad:
Button component with `
` and no ARIA attributes.
AntiPattern.WhyBad: A11y contract not fulfilled (`AX_A11Y_REQUIREMENTS`). Screen readers cannot identify the element as a button, cannot announce its label, cannot report its disabled state. Keyboard users cannot focus or activate it.
Override without audit trail (`AX_TOKEN_OVERRIDE_COMMENT`). Cannot distinguish intentional override from accidental drift. SpecValidator cannot trace the value back to the Token Mapping table.
AntiPattern.Good: `/* override: was 8px */` on the line above the declaration.

AntiPattern.WhyBad: Icon fill cannot be controlled via CSS when using `` (`AX_ICON_SVG_TIGHT_VIEWBOX`). Each appearance/mode combination would need a separate SVG file with hardcoded fill. Inlined SVG with `fill: currentColor` or CSS variable solves this.
AntiPattern.Good: Inline the SVG with tight viewBox; control fill via CSS on the `.icon` container or the SVG paths directly.

Hook.Purpose: Detect forbidden `:host()` pseudo-class usage in component CSS.
Hook.Expected: Empty output. Any match is a violation of `AX_DATA_ATTRS_NOT_HOST`.

Hook.Purpose: Verify every uikit component exposes `data-state` attribute.
Hook.Command: find src/ui -name '*.svelte' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print0 | xargs -0 grep -l 'data-state=' || echo MISSING
Hook.Expected: Every `.svelte` file in `src/ui/` must appear in output. `MISSING` means at least one component lacks `data-state`.

Hook.Purpose: Verify every uikit component has ARIA role and label.
Hook.Command: find src/ui -name '*.svelte' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print0 | xargs -0 grep -lE 'role="button"|role="checkbox"|role="radio"' || echo MISSING
Hook.Expected: Every `.svelte` file must appear. `MISSING` means a component lacks ARIA role.

Hook.Purpose: Verify all component files are in kebab-case.
Hook.Command: find src/ui -type f \( -name '*.svelte' -o -name '*.module.css' -o -name '*.tokens.css' -o -name '*.stories.ts' \) -not -path '*/node_modules/*' | while read f; do basename "$f"; done | grep -E '[A-Z]' || true
Hook.Expected: Empty output. Any match is a PascalCase/camelCase filename violation.

Hook.Purpose: Verify every override token has its audit comment.
Hook.Command: find src/ui -name '*.tokens.css' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print0 | xargs -0 grep -c 'override: was' || true
Hook.Expected: Count should match the number of OVERRIDE rows in the component's Token Mapping table. Zero when the spec has zero overrides; positive otherwise.

UiKitComponentSvelte.RewardCriteria:
âś… Component uses `data-*` attribute selectors on inner wrapper; no `:host()` anywhere. âś… CSS custom properties inherited through `:root` in `.tokens.css`; consumed via `var()` in `.module.css`. âś… Four files per component, all kebab-case: `.svelte`, `.module.css`, `.tokens.css`, `.stories.ts`. âś… `data-state` attribute present and used in CSS selectors for all interactive states. âś… ARIA role, label, disabled, and tabindex correctly wired. âś… SVG icons inlined with tight viewBox; fill controlled via CSS. âś… Override tokens carry `/* override: was ... */` comment. âś… Props match the spec's Variants table; `disabled` takes precedence over `state`. âś… Counter element has `box-sizing: border-box`.
âťŚ `:host()` selector in any CSS file. âťŚ Hardcoded pixel/color values in `.module.css` (must use `var()`). âťŚ PascalCase or camelCase component filenames. âťŚ Missing `data-state` attribute. âťŚ Missing ARIA role or label on interactive component. âťŚ Override token without audit comment. âťŚ Icon as `` tag (not inlined SVG). âťŚ `disabled` and `state` props conflicting without clear precedence.
